items.q: What is the main role of B-cells compared to T-cells?
items.correct: B-cells produce antibodies (humoral response); T-cells coordinate the immune response and kill infected cells (cell-mediated response).
items.misconception: B-cells and T-cells both produce antibodies in equal amounts.
items.random_wrong: T-cells produce antibodies and B-cells kill infected cells.
items.why: Students mix up which cell type does what; the B/T naming gives no intuitive clue to function.

items.q: What does insulin do to blood glucose?
items.correct: It lowers blood glucose by causing cells (especially liver and muscle) to take it up and store it as glycogen.
items.misconception: Insulin raises blood glucose to give the body energy.
items.random_wrong: Insulin destroys glucose in the bloodstream so it cannot be used.
items.why: Students associate sugar with energy and 'doing something with sugar' with raising energy, missing that insulin removes glucose from blood.

items.q: How do vaccines protect you from a disease?
items.correct: They expose your immune system to a harmless form of the pathogen so memory cells form, allowing rapid response if exposed later.
items.misconception: Vaccines kill the pathogen if it ever enters the body.
items.random_wrong: Vaccines provide antibodies for life by adding them directly to the blood.
items.why: Students think vaccines actively fight pathogens themselves, rather than priming the immune system to fight.

items.q: Which scientist proposed 'omnis cellula e cellula' (all cells from pre-existing cells)?
items.why: It's Virchow. “Schleiden” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: The fluid mosaic model of the cell membrane was proposed in 1972 by:
items.why: Correct answer: Singer and Nicolson. “Watson and Crick” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Virchow add 'omnis cellula e cellula' to cell theory?
items.why: The real figure is 1858 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
